extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions.

The only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out is true is t4 (on send_attempt if opted_out and acc==61). All other ways sent becomes true require not opted_out (t2), and the only transition that makes opted_out true (t1 optout) simultaneously sets sent:=false. t3, t5, t6, t7 all set sent:=false.

t4's guard requires acc==61, but acc==61 is unreachable. The step updates reduce (3*acc + k) modulo 64 (the nested up-to-3 subtractions of 64 equal mod 64 over the value range), with k in {48, 46, 44} for step_0/step_1/step_2 — all even. Since the initial acc=0 is even, and 3*even is even and adding an even constant keeps it even (and mod-64 reduction preserves parity because 64 is even), acc remains even forever. The reachable acc values are exactly the 32 even numbers {0,2,...,62}. 61 is odd, hence never reached, so t4 can never fire.

Exhaustive reachability over the full state space (opted_out, sent, acc) confirms this: 96 reachable states, none with opted_out=true and sent=true, and no reachable state has acc==61. Therefore the safety property is never violated within (and beyond) the 66-event bound.
